challenges.prompt: Complete this image tag so it has the two attributes every image needs: one that points to the file, and one that describes it.
challenges.explanation: The tag always needs src (where the image file is) and alt (what it shows). src makes the image appear; alt is read aloud by screen readers and displayed as a fallback when the file is missing or slow to load.

challenges.prompt: This code has a bug — what's wrong?
options: The alt text is not descriptive enough
is a void element, so it has no closing tag
src and alt should be swapped
It needs a type attribute like the <source> tag
challenges.explanation: The tag is a void element — it has no content of its own, so it never gets a closing tag. You just write the single opening tag with its attributes. The src and alt here are actually fine.

challenges.prompt: The HTML file lives in the folder 'site/'. The image file lives at 'site/images/photo.jpg'. What does this tag do in the browser?
options: The photo loads correctly from the images subfolder
The image is broken; the browser looks for photo.jpg next to the HTML file, but it is inside images/
The image loads but appears at zero width
The browser automatically searches every subfolder until it finds photo.jpg
challenges.explanation: A relative path with no folder means 'look next to the HTML file'. Since the image actually lives in the images/ subfolder, the correct path is src="images/photo.jpg". This mismatch is one of the most common causes of a broken-image icon.

challenges.prompt: Arrange these lines into a valid <audio> element that offers two formats and a fallback message.
lines: Your browser does not support audio.
challenges.explanation: The <audio> element opens with controls so the play/volume UI appears, then lists one or more <source> tags (offering mp3 and ogg improves browser compatibility), then the fallback text that shows only if no source works, and finally the closing </audio> tag.
